evals.prompt:
Here's my docker-compose.yml for a new project:

```yaml
services:
  db:
    image: postgres:18-alpine
    environment:
      POSTGRES_DB: myapp
      POSTGRES_USER: myuser
      POSTGRES_PASSWORD: secret
    volumes:
      - ./data/postgres:/var/lib/postgresql/data
    healthcheck:
      test: ['CMD-SHELL', 'pg_isready -U myuser']
      interval: 10s
      timeout: 5s
      retries: 5
    networks:
      - app-network
```

Is this correct?
evals.expected_output: Should identify that the volume mount target /var/lib/postgresql/data is WRONG for postgres:18-alpine, explain the PG18 VOLUME declaration change, and provide the corrected mount path /var/lib/postgresql

assertions.name: identifies-wrong-mount-path
assertions.text: The response clearly identifies that /var/lib/postgresql/data is the wrong mount target for postgres:18-alpine

assertions.name: provides-correct-path
assertions.text: The response provides the correct mount path: /var/lib/postgresql (without /data)

assertions.name: explains-silent-data-loss
assertions.text: The response explains or warns about the silent data loss risk (anonymous volumes, data not going to bind mount)

assertions.name: explains-pg18-change
assertions.text: The response explains that PostgreSQL 18 changed its VOLUME declaration

evals.prompt:
I'm upgrading my app from postgres:17-alpine to postgres:18-alpine. My current compose file has:

```yaml
  postgres:
    image: postgres:17-alpine
    volumes:
      - ./data/postgres:/var/lib/postgresql/data
```

I just changed the image tag to postgres:18-alpine. Anything else I need to update?
evals.expected_output: Should warn about two things: 1) the volume path needs to change from /var/lib/postgresql/data to /var/lib/postgresql for PG18, and 2) the user cannot just remount existing data — they need to pg_dump/pg_restore because PG18 uses a new on-disk format

assertions.name: flags-volume-path-change
assertions.text: The response identifies that the volume path must change for postgres:18-alpine

assertions.name: warns-about-data-migration
assertions.text: The response warns that existing PG17 data cannot be used directly by PG18 — a pg_dump/pg_restore is required

assertions.name: provides-migration-steps
assertions.text: The response provides concrete steps (pg_dumpall or pg_dump, then restore into fresh PG18)

evals.prompt:
My database keeps losing all its data every time I restart my containers. Here's the postgres service:

```yaml
  postgres:
    image: postgres:18
    restart: unless-stopped
    environment:
      - POSTGRES_DB=appdb
      - POSTGRES_USER=appuser
      - POSTGRES_PASSWORD=changeme
    volumes:
      - postgres_data:/var/lib/postgresql/data
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD-SHELL", "pg_isready -U appuser"]
      interval: 10s
      timeout: 5s
      retries: 5

volumes:
  postgres_data:
```

Why does data disappear?
evals.expected_output: Should identify that the named volume postgres_data is mounted to /var/lib/postgresql/data but PG18's VOLUME is declared at /var/lib/postgresql. Docker creates an anonymous volume at the real data path, so data goes to the anonymous volume, not postgres_data. Fix: change the mount target to /var/lib/postgresql

assertions.name: identifies-root-cause
assertions.text: The response correctly identifies that the volume target /var/lib/postgresql/data is wrong for postgres:18, causing data to go to an anonymous volume

assertions.name: provides-correct-fix
assertions.text: The response provides the fix: change postgres_data volume mount to /var/lib/postgresql

assertions.name: explains-mechanism
assertions.text: The response explains WHY data disappears — Docker VOLUME declaration vs bind/named mount path mismatch
